One coaching point that I just want to emphasize in this video, and this is because when I'm teaching, a common mistake I see or a technique issue that can be improved upon, let's say, is I find that the operator doesn't have the camera close enough
When I'm doing delicate dissection, the only part of the instruments I should see is the silver tips.
If you see the black shaft of the instrument when you're doing delicate dissection of pulmonary artery, pulmonary vein, you're probably not close enough.

You got to take advantage of the optics of the camera, the 10 times magnification, the stereo vision.
